name: Sessions and users by date
prompt: Show me sessions and active users for the last 7 days
expectations: Uses ga4 alias with the report subcommand
Includes -d date -m sessions,activeUsers
Uses --start 7daysAgo --end yesterday

name: Top pages by page views
prompt: Which pages got the most views last month?
expectations: Queries pagePath dimension with screenPageViews metric
Uses --order-by screenPageViews --desc
Applies a 30-day date range

name: Traffic sources breakdown
prompt: Where is our website traffic coming from?
expectations: Queries sessionSource and/or sessionMedium dimensions
Includes sessions metric
Uses report subcommand with appropriate date range

name: Filter by country
expectations: Uses --filter "country==Finland"
Applies the filter via the report subcommand

name: Multiple filters ANDed
prompt: Show sessions from Google organic traffic in Finland
expectations: Uses multiple --filter flags: country==Finland and sessionSource==google and sessionMedium==organic
Notes that multiple --filter flags are ANDed together

name: Realtime active users
prompt: How many users are on the site right now?
expectations: Uses ga4 realtime subcommand
Queries activeUsers metric
Notes that realtime covers approximately the last 30 minutes

name: First-time setup guidance
prompt: How do I set up the GA4 query tool?
expectations: Instructs to run setup.sh to create the virtual environment
Explains ga4-config.yaml creation with property_id and credentials_json
Mentions enabling Google Analytics Data API and Admin API in GCP Console
Provides test command: ga4 accounts

name: GA4 values are strings
prompt: Sum up the sessions from the report output
expectations: Notes that GA4 returns all values as strings, not numbers
Instructs to parse/convert strings before numeric aggregation

name: Property ID override
prompt: Query sessions for property 123456789 instead of the default
expectations: Uses --property 123456789 flag
Does not require editing the config file

name: List accounts and properties
prompt: What GA4 properties do we have access to?
expectations: Uses ga4 accounts subcommand
Returns list of accounts with their property summaries

name: Complex filter with JSON passthrough
expectations: Notes that OR logic requires --json passthrough since multiple --filter flags are ANDed
Constructs a JSON body with orGroup filterExpression

name: Date dimension format
prompt: The date in the results shows 20240115, what does that mean?
expectations: Explains that GA4 date dimension returns YYYYMMDD format (no dashes)
Provides the parsed date: 2024-01-15

name: Bounce rate vs engagement rate
prompt: Show bounce rate for our top landing pages
expectations: Queries landingPage dimension with bounceRate metric
Notes that bounceRate = 1 - engagementRate in GA4
